REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AGAINST THE ASSOCIATION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE ALL VIOLATIONS UNDER ONE CAUSE OF ACTION. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AGAINST INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH FACTS GIVING RISE TO A DECLARATORY RELIEF CLAIM AGAINST THESE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS. DEMURRER IS OTHERWISE OVERRULED. NOTHING ON THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT INDICATES THAT THE REQUEST FOR RELIEF UNDER CC 5145 IS MOOT. DECLARATION OF ARTHUR KEGELS IS STRICKEN. IMPROPER ON A DEMURRER. REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED. MOTION IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE REQUEST FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF TO BE PLEAD AS A REMEDY, NOT AS A SEPARATE CAUSE OF ACTION. MOTION IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO PRAYERS 2,3 AND 4 FOR PLAINTIFF TO TIE THESE PRAYERS TO THE SPECIFIC VIOLATIONS UPON WHICH THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS BASED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-01-2026 Continued To Jul-22-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER
Off Calendar May-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Plaintiff Stephen Kavanaugh appeared in pro per.
Lauren Holappa, Esq. representing Defendants via Zoom Video Webinar.

Hearing held.
Plaintiff argues by offer of proof and request for a continuance of the matter.
Defendants responds to Plaintiff's argument.

After hearing the arguments and requests, the Court takes the matter under submission.

Visiting Judge: Dennis Hayashi; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2026 Continued To May-27-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Feb-19-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Petitioner Stephen Kavanaugh in pro per.
Lauren Holappa, Esq. representing Respondent Douglass Manor Homeowners' Association via Zoom Video Webinar.

Hearing held.

The court has reviewed and considered all related pleadings submitted.
Petitioner argues as to the elections by offer of proof.
Respondent submit to the tentative rulings.
The matter is argued.

After hearing the arguments and offer of proof, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFEDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE

is GRANTED. Motion is granted with leave to amend as to the request for injunctive relief to be plead as a remedy, not as a separate cause of action. Motion is granted with leave to amend as to prayers 2,3 and 4 for Plaintiff to tie these prayers to the specific violations upon which the cause of action for declaratory relief is based.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Feb-19-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Petitioner Stephen Kavanaugh in pro per.
Lauren Holappa, Esq. representing Respondent Douglass Manor Homeowners' Association via Zoom Video Webinar.

Hearing held.

The court has reviewed and considered all related pleadings submitted.
Petitioner argues as to the elections by offer of proof.
Respondent submit to the tentative rulings.
The matter is argued.

After hearing the arguments and offer of proof, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER

is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to causes of action for declaratory relief against the association is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege all violations under one cause of action. Demurrer to the causes of action for declaratory relief against individual defendants is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts giving rise to a declaratory relief claim against these individual defendants. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Nothing on the face of the complaint indicates that the request for relief under CC 5145 is moot. Declaration of Arthur Kegels is stricken. Improper on a demurrer. Request for Judicial Notice is denied.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Declaration Of Stephen Kavanaugh
Off Calendar Sep-25-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances: Stephen Kavanaugh (415.522.1100) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona; Lauren Holappa of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney (no phone number provided) for defendant Etienne Lessard; No other appearances.

Due to a calendaring error, the court did not issue a tentative ruling on this motion. The court confirms that this motion is unopposed and rules as follows: Granted. Plaintiff's oral request to change the jurisdiction from Unlimited to Limited is not properly before the court. Plaintiff may file a motion to address that issue. A proposed order is submitted to the court for review. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)
